items.title: In vivo multimodal neurochemical interfaces for real-time decoding of brain circuit
items.author: Seongjun Park
items.content: Neural decoding remains constrained by methods that miss the brain’s chemical signalling dimension. In this Review, Kim and Park discuss hybrid and transformative neurochemical interfaces that provide real-time access to the brain’s dynamic molecular activity.

items.title: The brain–heart axis: effects of cardiovascular disease on the CNS and opportunities for central neuromodulation
items.author: Marmar Vaseghi
items.content: Maladaptive central autonomic remodelling that occurs in response to heart failure and hypertension drives sympathetic overactivity and cardiac dysfunction. In this Review, van Weperen and Vaseghi discuss the neural, glial and molecular mechanisms underlying these changes, as well as emerging neuromodulatory strategies aimed at restoring autonomic balance.

items.title: Time alone: cytokine-induced isolation during sickness
items.author: Sian Lewis
items.content: Across the animal kingdom, those who are sick self isolate. Here, this behaviour is shown — in mice — to be driven by systemic IL-1β release and mediated by an IL-1R1-expressing subpopulation of neurons in the dorsal raphe nucleus.

items.title: On the power of simple models: when emergent properties predict clinical outcomes
items.author: Erfan Nozari
items.content: In this Journal Club, Erfan Nozari discusses work detailing a simple, transparent algorithm for iEEG mapping of seizure onset zones and the emergent property of neural fragility at its core.

items.title: Synaptic-resolution connectomics: towards large brains and connectomic screening
items.author: Moritz Helmstaedter
items.content: Connectomics has delivered on its promise to map neuronal circuits at scale and at synaptic resolution. In this Review, Helmstaedter describes recent methodological achievements and remaining challenges in synaptic-resolution connectomics while synthesizing expanding connectomic mapping ambitions that include resolving local circuits of larger brains and screening of connectomes.

items.title: Author Correction: Autonomic dysfunction in neurodegenerative disease
items.author: Mara Mather
items.content: Author Correction: Autonomic dysfunction in neurodegenerative disease

items.title: The neuronal circuits and cellular encoding of thermosensation
items.author: James. F. A. Poulet
items.content: The thermosensory system across insects and mammals has shared principles of neuronal wiring and encoding. In this Review, Carta, Vestergaard and Poulet discuss how the nervous systems of insects and mammals process the thermal information that underlies thermal perception.

items.title: Learning in the overparametrized brain
items.author: N. Alex Cayco-Gajic
items.content: In this Journal Club, N. Alex Cayco-Gajic discusses a study published in 2004 that modelled the pyloric network of the lobster stomatogastric ganglion.
